summary: Top signals: JetBrains launches Kotlin benchmark for AI coding agents; Mistral debuts Vibe coding agent; Supabase showcases agentic coding with OpenCode; Google Gemini CLI preview released; Cloudflare, Cline, Claude Code, and OpenCode see active releases; Daytona and E2B each raise Series A for sandbox infrastructure; Peekaboo MCP server and Mastra framework gain traction. | Grok 4.5 pricing shocks coding agent market, GPT-5.6 launches with government approval, and agent sandboxing emerges as key infra fight. User workflows show multi-agent orchestration challenges and cost hacks. Security focus on A2A/MCP identity and audit trails.

candidates.title: JetBrains Kotlin Benchmark for AI Coding Agents
candidates.why_it_matters: New benchmark evaluates AI coding agents on real-world Kotlin tasks.

candidates.title: Mistral Launches Vibe Coding Agent
candidates.why_it_matters: Mistral launches coding agent for terminal, IDE, and background.

gaps: Missing user_workflow: No concrete operator field reports from social/discussion sources (Bluesky, Reddit, HN, X) available in this shard; only one user_workflow candidate (Supabase blog) found.
Missing mainstream_product: Could include more from OpenAI (Deutsche Telekom partnership) or Vercel AI SDK, but current selection covers multiple vendor families.
Missing infra_primitive: Only 3 included (Peekaboo, Mastra, E2B); additional options like browser-use, mem0, Modal, or Apify SDK were deferred to limit infra_primitive count in top 8.
Missing mainstream_product: Anthropic/Google/Microsoft/Cursor/GitHub changelog or release
Missing social/discussion: no Bluesky/Reddit/HN/X field reports for user_workflow with multiple corroborating sources
Missing infra_primitive: no new eval, memory, or deployment primitives beyond sandbox and MCP security
